item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm I'm not sure on this on my vote will stay neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Turns out, famine was right. This is the photo of a victim of the 1921-22 famine in Russia, who killed and ate his baby brother. Come on, guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, this is a parade of problems for me. It's also the quickest (tentative) vote decision I have made. I didnt think i would vote based on a picture, especially for meta reasons, but this photo is probably in very poor taste. I'll be damned if this is not the photo of a victim of a real tragedy. It's probably from a famine or genocide, and I don't like using such an image for our entertainment.

Also, I have to downvote for lack of cited references and for not detailing any of your methodology. These may not be expected in an SCP article, but I just read a peer reviewed biology article, not a containment procedure.

The sentence about identifying stage 1 people through combing relatives of known victims is so confusingly written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm having a hard time finding what's anomalous about this one. It's a prion that makes you horny in order to spread itself. Don't diseases do this all the time, just usually in the form of biting or similar means to spread through fluids? Where's the anomaly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's more than a creepy wunderwaffen. This is obviously some manner of effort to increase the productivity of the Lebensborn program in the short-term. Increase the sex drive of the best Aryan stock, decrease the amount of time it takes to birth a child, preferably while also speeding up the post-birth development rate, and you have the breeding stock and baseline of the Germanic-type peoples who'll begin the colonization process of everything between the river Vistula and Ural Mountains. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like this one, particularly the tone. At first it seems kind of bland, but then you see it's basically a creepy wunderwaffen from the Nazi last stand, which really, REALLY works for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit young? You mean, not looking like taken during the 40s, or that the subject appears young?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: picture looks a bit young, but upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture makes it impossible for me to evaluate this article on its merits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's almost like the writing is too scientific to convey the emotional impact of this thing. This could be very gut-wrenching, but I'm wading through too MUCH chemistry and biology.

I love the science that you've got in this thing. It's great to see well-researched concepts, but it may just be more than you need.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not feeling this one. The concept's a bit bland, as is the execution. Neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I don't know, either =^.^;=

How about a 'homogenous consolidation of prions'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have the feeling that there must be some sort of technical term that would be used for a group of infectious agents acting in concert, rather than "mixture", but I have no idea what it would be.

The only words I can quickly find that come close are "polymicrobial" and "coinfection", but it's dubious if they can really be associated with prions, as they are not microbes and technically in this case it's one infection caused by a cocktail of prions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have the feeling that there must be some sort of technical term that would be used for a group of infectious agents acting in concert, rather than "mixture", but I have no idea what it would be.

Yeah, I don't know, either =^.^;=

Why did he suspect that Novosadov's behaviour was anomalous? Did the intercepted WWII message lead to a standing order to be alert for serial rapists who had any connection to Nazi Germany research facilities?

Hmm, you got me thinking here, because what I wrote made less obvious sense than what I thought.
You are right - the depeche did call for a similar treatment of anyone showing similar symptoms - this was after the key content from the facility was found and hauled away - I'm imagining the second even more thorough emptying and demolition was more of an afterthought when they found that the other side knows more than they expected they do - so they got data from german experiments, and I imagine it was a very headdesk moment for the Division P folks when they realised the protection the soldiers hauling the stuff away apparently wasn't adequate as well, at least one showed what were now understood as symptoms.

From the Foundation's side, I suspect they got somewhat lucky in that the agent in question decided to take samples to be sure. I suspect what happened is that this soldier, who they knew was one of those involved in grabbing some german research showed up in a base the Foundation had (clearly undiscovered) people in, and before they could talk to him , or haul him away for information (In my headcanon , at the time amnesiacs were a new thing, and Foundation was the first to get them - he'd have just disappeared and showed up again confused) to get at least the little bit he potentially knew, he disappeared, and was brought back in the state as the photo shows, shot, and then there was the order you mention.

I hope this makes at least some sense, and that I unwittingly didn't introduce a shovelful more of headbangers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1232 is the designation given to a mixture of misfolded variants of [REDACTED] proteins (prions),

I have the feeling that there must be some sort of technical term that would be used for a group of infectious agents acting in concert, rather than "mixture", but I have no idea what it would be.

Agent Golovanov4, by the time infiltrated within the Soviet army as a telegraphist, has managed to capture a coded depeche ordering the prompt incineration of Private Novosadov's remains, and was able to obtain tissue samples and a copy of the autopsy report.

Why did he suspect that Novosadov's behaviour was anomalous? Did the intercepted WWII message lead to a standing order to be alert for serial rapists who had any connection to Nazi Germany research facilities?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because that's what I thought it'd be termed as.
I'm not native english, and occassionally fuck up, still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pardon the question, but why use the French word for "dispatch" in this context?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was kind of going "Wha?" the entire time, but the letter gave it all purpose. Which isn't to say that's the best way this could have been done, but it's effective if you read the whole thing. Also scary that they just know how to make it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well.. does either of you have a better picture in mind? I'm open to suggestions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having read through the whole thing I like the idea a lot, but the picture makes me uncomfortable. It certainly suits the piece well, but I just don't like knowing the truth behind a picture like that and it takes me away from the fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Cough*

I did not derp there. Move along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It spends too much time on scientific details that I don't see as important to the central idea. I have a general idea of the final product but feel like it could've been better served by approaching it from a different angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
German, but the Russians have seized it before the Foundation could.
Also your comment makes me all warm and fuzzy =^.^=

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amazing, even more so that it was a secret German weapon to gain more soldiers.

Upboated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this has something to do with something anomalous that's happening there, and believe me. the sacs hang there for a reason.
That said, yeah, I should thank Hornby as the rape prion was part of what inspired this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loads (giggity) better than the homo-hating sex prion posted last month.

second month where the placentae [DATA EXPUNGED] sacs.

This expungement reads a little goofy, at least for someone not intimate with natal progression. Is there a better way to do this so 'sacs' doesn't just hang off the end of the sentence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We discussed this in skype. Upboated.
